INITIAL REPORT OF TORNADO ON LOWER RED LAKE WHICH QUICKLY MOVED ONSHORE AT RED LAKE AND LIKELY TRACKED FOR LESS THAN A MILE, ENDING BY 515 PM CDT. DAMAGING DOWNBURST WIND, HEAVY RAIN AND LARGE HAIL CONTINUED TO TRACK SOUTHEASTWARD WITH THIS STORM. THE TORNADO TORE LARGE AREAS OF ROOFING MATERIAL AND TRIM OFF OF THE TRIBAL COLLEGE AND OTHER BUILDINGS AND HOMES. A LIGHT POLE AND SIGNS WERE TOPPLED. NUMEROUS LARGE PINE AND OAK TREES WERE SNAPPED. MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 300 YARDS. PEAK WINDS TO 95 MPH.
PRELIMINARY REPORT OF OBSERVED TORNADO. REPORTS OF SOME STRUCTURAL DAMAGE.

HAIL UP TO TENNIS BALL SIZED, AND DAMAGING DOWNBURST WINDS UP TO 70 MPH, PUMMELLED THE AREA BETWEEN THE COMMUNITIES OF RED LAKE AND REDBY, AND NEARBY TRIBAL LANDS IMMEDIATELY SOUTH AND SOUTHEAST OF THESE COMUNITIES. WIDESPREAD SHINGLE AND ROOFING DAMAGE WAS EVIDENT, ALONG WITH HOME AND VEHICLE WINDOWS SHATTERED. LARGE TREE LIMBS WERE ALSO BLOWN DOWN THROUGHOUT THE AREA, AND NUMEROUS LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED.

THIS TORNADO FORMED NEAR THE SHORE OF LOWER RED LAKE AND TRACKED TO THE SOUTHEAST. IT CROSSED MN HWY 1 NEAR THE INTERSECTION WITH BIA15 AND PENETRATED DEEPLY IN TO A FOREST OF LARGE EVERGREEN AND OAK TREES. TREES WERE SNAPPED AND DEBRIS TANGLED, THOUGH MOST DEBRIS TRACKED WITH THE SOUTHEASS MOVING STORM. THE TORNADO APPEARED TO HAVE DISSIPATED AROUND 522 PM CDT, AFTER CROSSING BIA18, ROUGHLY 6 MILES ESE OF REDBY. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 95 MILES AN HOUR. MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 300 YARDS.

THIS TORNADO WAS LIKELY WARPPED IN DOWNBURST WINDS, HEAVY RAIN, AND LARGE HAIL. THE TORNADO CORSSED SEVERAL COUNTY AND TOWNSHIP ROADS AS IT TRACKED TO THE SOUTHEAST, WITH NUMEROUS POPLAR AND OAK TREES SNAPPED ALONG ITS PATH. THE TORNADO APPEARS TO HAVE CROSS PORTIONS OF DARK LAKE, WHITEFISH LAKE, AND SANDY LAKE IN NEBISH AND HAGALI TOWNSHIPS, BEFORE DISSIPATING NEAR THE WEST EDGE OF GULL LAKE, ABOUT 4 WNW TENSTRIKE, BY 536 PM CDT. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 80MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 200 YARDS.

A TORNADO TRACKED FOR ABOUT 3 MILES TO AROUND 5 NNE OF FISHER BY 1101 PM CDT. IT CARRIED DEBRIS TO THE EAST AND SOUTHEAST FOR UP TO 500 YARDS WHIL IT MAINLY TRACKED TO THE EAST. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 105 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 200 YARDS.

A TORNADO TRACKED INTERMITTENTLY FOR NEARLY TWO MILES FROM JUST NORTH OF HWY 13 THROUGH THE BRUNS SLOUGH AREA OF CENTRAL DENVER TOWNSHIP, TO AROUND 5 NNE OF CRETE BY 408 PM CDT. THE TORNADO REPEATEDLY SHOWED A DUST PLUME BUT STAYED MAINLY OVER OPEN GRASSLAND. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 70 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 25 YARDS.
GROUND CIRCULATIONS ALSO REPORTED FROM FAIRMONT TO WAHPETON WITH NO CONDENSATION FUNNELS.

FUNNEL CLOUD SPOTTED OVER EAGLE LAKE, DAMAGE ON SHORE WITH FLIPPED PONTOONS AND TREES DOWN.

NWS STORM DAMAGE SURVEY OF NORTHWEST SHORE OF EAGLE LAKE 3 MILES WEST OF FRAZEE REVEALED TWO UPROOTED 18-24 INCH PINE TREES AND A FLIPPED DOCK. ANOTHER DOCK HAD THREE ALUMINUM DECK PANELS RIPPED FROM IT. EYEWITNESS TO EVENT STATED TREES FELL AND ALUMINUM DECK PANELS FLEW IN THE SAME DIRECTION INDICATING STRAIGHT-LINE MICROBURST WINDS. ONE TREE CAUSED A SLIGHT INDENTATION OF ASPHALT DRIVEWAY. EVALUATION OF DAMAGE INDICATORS RESULTED IN ESTIMATED 75 TO 90 MPH WINDS.
